beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Jobsekr's ambition goes beyond aggregating listings to building an intelligence layer on top. That layer is a retrieval-augmented generation system for career intelligence. Not all PDFs are equal, so document quality directly shapes what retrieval can surface. Two key decisions drive the build: how documents are handled and which model is chosen.

artifactLines: Jobsekr's ambition goes beyond aggregating listings to building an intelligence layer on top.
That layer is a retrieval-augmented generation system for career intelligence.
Not all PDFs are equal, so document quality directly shapes what retrieval can surface.
Two key decisions drive the build: how documents are handled and which model is chosen.

beats.narration_text: The line that ambition doesn't stop at aggregation, that an intelligence layer sits on top and that not all PDFs are equal, is exactly the RAG problem I want to understand. Help me go deeper: (1) explain what the retrieval-augmented generation intelligence layer adds beyond simply collecting job listings; (2) why messy or unequal PDFs sabotage retrieval quality and how to fix that; (3) how the 'second was the model' choice interacts with retrieval to shape final answer quality for a job seeker.
